meta.whyDifferent: The game's first Firecast cantrip. Every shipped Firecast card (Searing_Ray, Combust, Cauterize, Fireball, Evangelize, Faith_Healing, ...) trades the buried card for effect; none replaces itself. Burying Emberseed draws mid-bury, so mass-bury effects (Deification bury:3, Awakening bury:10, Torch) stop costing deck size — a new setup for the bury archetype. Also a deliberate graveyard-filler: Firecast cards land in the graveyard after bury-cast (SpellEffects.BuryCard), feeding this pack's graveyard payoffs.
meta.intendedDeck: Any bury or Firecast deck; also splashable in graveyard-count decks as fuel. Play it early for a chip cantrip, or leave it in the deck as a pleasant bury hit.
meta.powerNotes: 0-cost common budget ~2-3 dmg-equiv (spec §4). 1 Burning (never decays, ~1 dmg/turn) + cantrip ≈ Evangelize (0-cost: 1 Zeal + random blessing). inflict:X + referenceStatus Burning idiom per Fireball.json; draw:1 ubiquitous.

meta.whyDifferent: Ports the Ancestral gate (CardsInGraveyard > 3, exact condition 11|IsMoreThan|3 from Boneskewer.json/Blood_Ritual.json) out of the DEXSTR shaman suite into the INT burn suite for the first time. Every shipped burn-scaling card keys off cardsBuried (Ashes_to_Ashes) or existing Burning (Fan_the_Flames); none reads the discard pile, so burn decks today are indifferent to their graveyard. This makes the burn deck want a stocked graveyard — synergy with Firecast casts landing there and tension with bury (which exiles instead of filling).
meta.intendedDeck: INT burn deck with discard/Firecast fill; turns on around turn 3-4. Anti-synergy with heavy bury-exile lines is deliberate texture.
meta.powerNotes: 1-cost common baseline 3-5 dmg (spec §4). Flat 3 + conditional 4 Burning (repeating, never decays per Burning.json) is above rate only once Ancestral is on, mirroring Boneskewer's setup discount (C, 1+Life, dmg+bleed+ancestral escalation).

cards.name: Gravemoss
cards.description: Gain 2 Armor. Bury a card. When Buried: Gain 5 Armor.

meta.whyDifferent: First player-pool card whose own Bury-trigger effect is the payoff (Suntree_Twig, the only shipped card with a self Bury trigger, uses it for a hidden transform, and it's a Grounded basic). SpellEffects.BuryCard runs a buried card's Bury-trigger CardEffectList before filing it — verified engine path. This is 'Firecast for defense' without the keyword: you rig your own random bury:1 hits so the deck becomes a lottery you want to lose cards to. New decision: the card is better buried than played.
meta.intendedDeck: Bury-dense decks (Prayer/Singe/Torch shells). Play it when you need the bury trigger now; leave it in the deck when you're chaining buries.
meta.powerNotes: 0-cost common ~2-3 value: 2 Armor + bury enabler in hand (cf. Singe: 0-cost, 2 Burning + bury). Buried case pays 5 Armor at a random time — comparable to one Bone_Ritual hit. Armor fades at StartPhase (Armor.json) so buried procs during your turn are full-value, procs off enemy-turn buries decay.

cards.name: Everburning Censer
cards.description: Inflict 3 Burning. Persistent. When Buried: Inflict 3 Burning.

meta.whyDifferent: The first bury-payload card that survives burial. SpellEffects.BuryCard routes Persistent cards to the graveyard instead of exile — a verified interaction no shipped card is built around (Persistent ships only as a hand-retention keyword). So every burial of the Censer burns the foe AND stocks the graveyard with a retrievable card; return effects (Recycle, Roll_the_Bones, this pack's Emberdredge) loop it. Singe merely enables bury; this one is a repeatable bury target.
meta.intendedDeck: Bury+recursion hybrid: bury it from the deck for 3 Burning, dig it back out of the graveyard, replay it. Persistent also keeps it in hand across turns.
meta.powerNotes: 1-cost common: 3 Burning (~3/turn repeating, never decays) sits at the ~4 dmg/energy line as delayed damage; cf. Halo_of_Wrath's 3 Burning per bury and Sanctify's 3 Burning at HOLY 3 with a heal rider. Bury proc is another 3 only when a bury effect happens to hit it in the deck.

cards.name: Flameletting
cards.description: Gain 2 Burning. Deal damage equal to twice your Burning.

meta.whyDifferent: First card that WANTS Burning on yourself — the spec's example of a new mode (a payoff for a status on self). Shipped self-Burning is only ever a cost rider (Cauterize) or something to cleanse (Aura_of_Fire, Fire_Resistance). Because Burning never decays (Burning.json clearAt None), each copy escalates: play 1 = 4 dmg, play the second at 4 self-stacks = 8+. Creates a masochist INT line where Zeal's fire upkeep, Cauterize's rider and cleanse timing become deck decisions.
meta.intendedDeck: INT fire deck with heal/cleanse release valves (Cauterize's 4 heal, Fire_Resistance, Aura_of_Fire). Race the foe before your own pyre wins.
meta.powerNotes: Turn-1 floor: 2 self-Burning -> 4 dmg for 1 INT (par), while eating 2/turn thereafter — the escalation upside is paid for by permanent self-DoT. bless:X + referenceStatus Burning self-application verified in Cauterize.json; [[my(status)Burning]] token verified in Pyroclasm.json; *2 base-damage arithmetic is NumberParser int math, NOT the multiplydamage:2 crit channel.

cards.name: Chosen for the Pyre
cards.description: Bury a card of your choice from your deck. Inflict Burning equal to twice its cost.

meta.whyDifferent: Censorship is the only shipped choose-bury card and pays defense (Zeal + heal, HOLY identity). This ports precision burial to the INT burn suite and pays damage — and the choice bites differently: exiling a junk 0-cost fixes your deck but burns for nothing; burying your expensive bomb burns big but costs the card (unless it's Firecast, which casts instead — pointing it at Buried Sun is the dream line). New decision on an existing verified structure.
meta.intendedDeck: INT burn deck that wants both deck-thinning and reach; combos with Firecast payloads since buryselect on a Firecast card casts it AND still reads its cost.
meta.powerNotes: Structure (enchant:self + buryselect, EndAction payload reading [[lastBuriedEnergy]], removeenchant:this) copied 1:1 from Censorship.json; play condition CardsInDeckRemaining>0 likewise. Burning 2x cost (avg ~2-4 stacks) vs Censorship's Zeal 2x + heal 2x at Uncommon — comparable value, offense-shifted.

cards.name: Emberdredge
cards.description: Return a card from your discard pile to your hand, then inflict Burning equal to its cost.

meta.whyDifferent: Bone_Ritual (Rare, DEXSTR+Life, Armor = 2x cost) is the only shipped card that reads the returned card's cost; this reverses the pack's signature bridge — where Firecast turns burial into casts (deck->fire), this turns retrieval into burn (graveyard->fire). It also creates a priced-retrieval decision: digging back a cheap cantrip burns little, digging back Buried Sun or another bomb burns big — recursion that advances the burn clock instead of stalling it. Not strictly better than Recycle (Recycle chooses at Common with no INTSTR requirement; identical retrieval, no rider, different color).
meta.intendedDeck: Burn/graveyard hybrid — the pack's centerpiece glue. Loops Everburning Censer and re-arms Buried Sun.
meta.powerNotes: Recycle (C, DEXSTR1) = bare return:choose; +1x-cost Burning rider at Uncommon INTSTR mirrors Bone_Ritual's rarity-stepped rider (its 2x Armor costs Rare + 1 Life). [[lastpickedenergy]] after return:choose + EndAction-enchant pattern verified verbatim in Bone_Ritual.json.

cards.name: Ashen Harvest
cards.description: Deal damage equal to the cards in your discard pile ([[cardsInGraveyard]]). Reaping.

meta.whyDifferent: Rapture spends the graveyard count as attack COUNT (2 dmg x N, lifedrain, 3-cost); this is one scaling hit with Reaping, so the payout is permanent max-HP shred (SpellEffects.Damage Reaping block) — no shipped card combines graveyard-count scaling with Reaping. It also creates the pack's core tension: return effects (Emberdredge, Recycle) SHRINK this card's damage, so you choose between recursion and the harvest. Heavy cards are ideal fuel — they never leave the graveyard on reshuffle (DeckHandler.ReshuffleGraveyard).
meta.intendedDeck: DEXSTR graveyard deck stocking Heavy/Firecast cards; finisher vs high-HP bosses where max-HP loss compounds with Grave_Pact-style effects.
meta.powerNotes: 2-cost par ~8 dmg (spec §4). Graveyard is typically 5-10 by mid-combat; Uncommon buys the Reaping rider against the slow start and the self-imposed anti-recursion tension. [[cardsInGraveyard]] in a damage slot verified in Rapture.json/Soultap.json.

cards.name: Cinder Archive
cards.description: This combat, whenever your foe takes Burning damage, return a random card from your discard pile to your hand.

enchantment.text: Whenever your foe takes Burning damage, return a random card from your discard pile to your hand.

meta.whyDifferent: Sanctify proves the trigger (InflictStatusDamage + DamageType Is fire = per-Burning-tick hook) and pays healing; Roll_the_Bones returns 1 random card per turn unconditionally. This card is neither: it converts burn tick FREQUENCY into card advantage, so Combust-style forced ticks and Fan_the_Flames doubling become draw engines. No shipped card links the burn and graveyard resources at all — this is the archetype handshake the cluster is missing.
meta.intendedDeck: Burn deck splashing graveyard payoffs, or graveyard deck adopting a Burning subtheme. Baseline 1 card/turn off the EndTurnPhase tick; spikes with Combust chains.
meta.powerNotes: Compare Roll_the_Bones (Rare, DEXSTR1, 1 random return per turn flat). Same rarity/cost, requires Burning online, but scales past 1/turn with tick effects. Trigger+condition pair verified in Sanctify.json (78 + 70|Is|fire). UNVERIFIED nuance: whether ticks of Burning on YOURSELF (Zeal upkeep, Flameletting) also fire the player-side InflictStatusDamage scan — Sanctify's extra EnemyTurn/CurrentPhase guards suggest direction-handling subtleties; if self-ticks proc it, that is an accepted (noted) synergy, not a break. Sim-harness check requested.

cards.name: Cinder Cycle
cards.description: This combat, whenever your deck reshuffles, inflict 6 Burning and gain 6 Armor.

enchantment.text: Whenever your deck reshuffles, inflict 6 Burning and gain 6 Armor.

meta.whyDifferent: The Reshuffle trigger exists on exactly three shipped assets, all enemy-side or system (Entropy, Divine_Ire, Fatigue) — no player card pays off reshuffling. This makes the fatigue race a strategy: bury-thinning, Gravesite and Orphic_Glance dig you toward reshuffles that now pay 6 Burning + 6 Armor while fatigue still ticks (second reshuffle onward hurts ~10% maxHP), a self-balancing greed engine. Verified trigger/effect-host combination no shipped player card uses.
meta.intendedDeck: Thin bury deck or Gravesite mill deck that plans 2-4 reshuffles per long fight; the Armor half offsets fatigue chip.
meta.powerNotes: Reshuffles happen ~1-2x naturally, 3-4x when built for; ~2-3 activations of 6+6 for 1 energy at Rare is in line with Roll_the_Bones-class engines. Reshuffle trigger (GameTriggers 54) hosting enchantment effects verified in Entropy.json/Divine_Ire.json/Fatigue.json. Interaction with Emberweave Communion's reshuffle-bury flagged for sim.

meta.whyDifferent: First Firecast+Heavy card in the pool (verified: zero shipped cards carry keywords 11+21 together). The keywords interlock into a lifecycle no card has: bury it and it detonates free (Firecast), then parks in the graveyard permanently (Heavy survives every reshuffle, DeckHandler.ReshuffleGraveyard) as fuel for Ashen Harvest/Ashfeast — until Emberdredge digs it out for a paid second detonation. Hand-cast is the fair fallback, one shot per cycle. Not strictly better than Elite_Fireball (Legendary, INT1, 8 dmg + burn, no Heavy): this costs more and carries a real drawback.
meta.intendedDeck: Any deck in this cluster: burn decks want the payload, bury decks want the roulette hit, graveyard decks want the permanent resident.
meta.powerNotes: 2-cost Rare envelope 5-8 dmg (spec §4): 8 + 4 Burning is top-of-band, paid for by Heavy (once per combat without recursion) — mirrors Grave_Pact's Heavy-as-rate-payment (U, DEXSTR1, escalating steal). Firecast bury-cast path verified in SpellEffects.BuryCard (PlayAction effects execute, card -> graveyard).

cards.name: Emberweave Communion
cards.description: Whenever you Bury a card, inflict Burning equal to 2 plus its cost. Whenever your deck reshuffles, Bury 3 cards.

enchantment.text: Whenever you Bury a card, inflict Burning equal to 2 plus its cost. Whenever your deck reshuffles, Bury 3 cards.

meta.whyDifferent: Halo_of_Wrath pays a flat 3 Burning per bury; this scales by the buried card's cost ([[lastBuriedEnergy]] on the Bury trigger — player-positive use of the Extraction_Listener idiom, which ships only as an enemy affliction) AND closes the loop: reshuffles auto-bury 3, and burying accelerates the next reshuffle. The deck literally burns itself down for damage, racing fatigue — an engine, not a rate card, and the only card that welds this pack's two Rare engines (Cinder Cycle, bury payoffs) into one identity.
meta.intendedDeck: All-in bury inferno: Prayer/Singe/Deification density, Firecast payloads so burials replace themselves, Cinder Cycle for the reshuffle turns. Wincon is Burning snowball before fatigue or deck-death.
meta.powerNotes: Per bury: 2+cost (~3 avg) Burning ≈ Halo_of_Wrath (Common) per-proc, so the Legendary premium is entirely the reshuffle clause and scaling ceiling. DANGER FLAGGED for sim: reshuffle->bury:3 can chain (BuryCard calls ResetDeck when the deck empties mid-bury, firing Reshuffle again); loop terminates via NO_MORE_CARDS/Heavy-only graveyard but each cycle adds fatigue — intended as a self-limiting greed spiral; verify no lock.

weapons.name: Cinderbough Wand
weapons.description: Deal [damage:2] fire damage. If a card was buried this combat, apply 1 Burning.

meta.nearestExisting: Forcewand.json (statline twin: 2 elemental damage + archetype rider); Warmace.json (only shipped weapon applying a status)
meta.whyDifferent: Every shipped weapon's rider is positional or unconditional (Ambush, Flanking, Cascade, always-on). Cinderbough Wand is the first whose rider reads run/combat state from an archetype (CardsBurned > 0) - once any bury has happened this combat, the repeating basic attack becomes a burn engine. It closes the pack's missing graveyard->burn direction (DESIGN-NOTES gap map) from turn 1 of the run.
meta.intendedDeck: EmberweaveGrove bury shells: Emberseed/Gravemoss enable it by turn 2; Combust/Flameletting read the Burning it accrues.
meta.powerNotes: Statline par: damage 2 at 1 Neutral, Common - identical to 5 of 6 shipped weapons. Rider = 1 Burning/turn, gated behind a real setup (a bury) exactly like Warmace's ungated 2 Brittle/turn at cost 2; conditional 1/turn at cost 1 is under that shipped ceiling.

weaponPowers.name: Cindersong Weapon
weaponPowers.description: Use your weapon power to inflict Burning equal to your level ([[myLevel]]) at the start of next combat. Passive: Whenever you Bury a card, inflict 1 Burning.

meta.nearestExisting: Flaming Weapon (cd3: Burning [[myLevel]] + Focus [[myLevel]] + Fire Immunity talent)
meta.whyDifferent: Flaming Weapon pays its second half in Focus + a defensive talent; Cindersong pays it in a bury-conditional in-combat rider, making every bury effect (the pack's whole engine) also a burn application. No shipped tier-0 talent listens on the Bury trigger.
meta.intendedDeck: EmberweaveGrove bury decks; the rider turns Emberseed/Buried Sun loops into repeated 1-Burning pings.
meta.powerNotes: cd3 curve point: shipped cd3 = level-scaled status (Feral OLD Bleeding 2xlvl) or a start-of-combat card. Burning 1xlvl (vs Flaming's identical primary) + a conditional rider that needs deck support sits at/under Flaming's total package. UNVERIFIED: Bury as a talent rider trigger has no shipped tier-0 precedent (legal GameTriggers member; rider-on-nonstandard-trigger pattern is shipped, e.g. Shaking Weapon on ShuffleCard).

weaponPowers.name: Pyregraft Weapon
weaponPowers.description: Use your weapon power to add the Firecast keyword to a card in your deck and start next combat with 1 Zeal.

meta.nearestExisting: Psychic Weapon (cd4: addkeywordindeck:psionic + startstatus Focus [[myLevel]] + Brainrot passive)
meta.whyDifferent: Same permanent-keyword chassis, opposite archetype: Psionic converts costs to Focus, Firecast converts burial into casting - it manufactures the pack's core enabler one card at a time, something no shipped power or card does for Firecast.
meta.intendedDeck: Any Ember deck: graft Firecast onto a big INT action, then bury it with Gravemoss/Chosen for the Pyre for a free cast.
meta.powerNotes: cd4 curve point: permanent single-card deck edits live at cd4 (Psychic, Coiling, Forging). Deliberately lighter than Psychic (flat 1 Zeal vs level-scaled Focus, no passive talent) because Firecast on the right card is a repeatable free cast every reshuffle. UNVERIFIED: addkeywordindeck ships only with the psionic argument; firecast is a legal CardProperties member parsed by the same enum path - flagged for in-game QA.

weaponPowers.name: Hearthguard Weapon
weaponPowers.description: Use your weapon power to start next combat with Armor equal to twice your level ([[myLevel]]*2) and inflict 1 Burning on your foe.

meta.nearestExisting: Fortified Weapon (cd2: Armor [[myLevel]]*3 + Heavy Armor card)
meta.whyDifferent: Fortified is pure turtle; Hearthguard trades a third of the armor and the free card for an offensive splash that seeds the pack's Burning payoffs - the only shipped-pool-adjacent power that preps both sides of the board.
meta.intendedDeck: Knight INTSTR bridge decks (Cinder Archive / Emberdredge); the Burning pip turns on foe-is-Burning conditions from turn 1.
meta.powerNotes: cd2 curve point: one small level-scaled next-combat status package (Fortified 3xlvl Armor + card; Baneful 2xlvl Poison + passive). Armor 2xlvl + 1 Burning is strictly inside that envelope - both codeLines are shipped-verbatim patterns (Fortified/Baneful startstatus forms).
